# Personal Recipe Collection

## Overview

This collection belongs to Nina and Alex Petrova, a family of four (two adults, two children ages 6 and 10) living in Seattle, WA. The collection focuses on Mediterranean and Asian cuisines, with an emphasis on meal prep efficiency and feeding a family on a reasonable budget.

## Philosophy

We prioritize from-scratch cooking using whole ingredients. We avoid ultra-processed foods and try to source ingredients locally when practical. Our regular grocery stores are PCC Community Markets (for produce and specialty items) and Costco (for bulk staples). We supplement with online orders from Burlap & Barrel (spices) and King Arthur Baking (flour and baking supplies).

## Weekly Routine

- **Sunday**: Batch cook proteins (roast chicken, marinate and grill tofu), prepare grains (rice, quinoa), wash and chop vegetables for the week
- **Monday-Friday**: Assemble meals from prepped components, cook one fresh dish per evening (usually 30-45 minutes)
- **Saturday**: Baking day — sourdough bread, sometimes pizza dough or pastries

## Budget

Target grocery spend: $150/week ($600/month) for a family of 4. This averages to $5.36 per person per day. We track spending in a shared Google Sheet. In 2025, we averaged $162/week, slightly over budget due to rising dairy and egg prices.

## Dietary Notes

- Alex has a mild lactose intolerance — uses lactose-free milk for drinking but tolerates aged cheeses and yogurt
- Daughter Mila (age 6) is a picky eater — will eat pasta, rice, chicken, and most fruits. Working on expanding vegetable acceptance.
- Son Leo (age 10) will eat almost anything and is interested in helping cook
- No other allergies or dietary restrictions

## Collection Organization

Recipes are organized by type: main recipes, techniques, and ingredients reference. Each recipe includes source attribution, prep/cook times, and serving sizes.
